evidence.text: Control Products & Solutions sales decreased 9.6 percent year over year in the three months ended December 31, 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is clearly decelerating with a significant year-over-year decrease in sales.

evidence.text: Organic sales decreased 3.8 percent year over year in the three months ended December 31, 2015, and currency translation reduced sales by 5.8 percentage points.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with a 3.8% year-over-year decrease and additional negative impact from currency translation.

evidence.text: Product sales decreased 7 percent in the three months ended December 31, 2015 compared to the three months ended December 31, 2014.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining by 7% year over year, indicating a clear negative trend.

factors.summary: Operating margins are stable despite lower sales.

evidence.text: Segment operating margin increased to 15.3 percent in the three months ended December 31, 2015 from 14.5 percent a year ago despite lower sales, primarily due to productivity.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins have improved despite lower sales, driven by productivity gains.

evidence.text: Segment operating margin increased to 15.3 percent in the three months ended December 31, 2015 from 14.5 percent a year ago despite lower sales, primarily due to productivity.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements have led to a significant increase in operating margins, despite lower sales.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are unfavorably impacting sales.

evidence.text: Currency translation reduced sales by 5.8 percentage points in the three months ended December 31, 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements have unfavorably impacted sales, reducing them by 5.8 percentage points.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is declining.

evidence.text: Free cash flow was $145.3 million for the three months ended December 31, 2015 compared to $232.6 million for the three months ended December 31, 2014.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has declined significantly year over year, indicating a negative trend in cash generation.
